root_cause.text: The lack of validation on archive entry names during extraction allows an attacker to include directory traversal sequences (e.g., `../`) in filenames, potentially writing files outside of the intended target directory (Zip Slip vulnerability).
root_cause.evidence: The patch adds checks in `RunJar.unJar()` to ensure that the canonical path of the extracted file starts with the canonical path of the target directory (`!file.getCanonicalPath().startsWith(targetDirPath)`), and the added test case explicitly attempts to extract an entry named `../outside.path`.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The lack of validation on archive entry names during extraction allows an attacker to include directory traversal sequences (e.g., `../`) in filenames, potentially writing files outside of the intended target directory (Zip Slip vulnerability).
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch adds checks in `RunJar.unJar()` to ensure that the canonical path of the extracted file starts with the canonical path of the target directory (`!file.getCanonicalPath().startsWith(targetDirPath)`), and the added test case explicitly attempts to extract an entry named `../outside.path`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add security guard to prevent Zip Slip directory traversal vulnerabilities during jar extraction.
intent_record.what: Added a canonical path check to ensure the extracted file is within the target directory.
intent_record.how: Prevents directory traversal attacks by validating that the resolved path of the jar entry does not escape the intended destination directory.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add target directory canonical path variable to support path traversal (Zip Slip) prevention during jar extraction.
intent_record.what: Added a variable 'targetDirPath' that computes the canonical path of the target directory.
intent_record.how: Pre-computes the target directory path, setting up the necessary state to validate that extracted jar entries do not escape the destination directory.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a security guard to prevent path traversal during jar extraction.
intent_record.what: Added a canonical path check to ensure the extracted file path starts with the target directory path.
intent_record.how: Prevents path traversal attacks (Zip Slip) by throwing an IOException if a jar entry attempts to write outside the intended destination directory.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add static import for JUnit's fail method to support testing.
intent_record.what: Added a static import for org.junit.Assert.fail.
intent_record.how: Makes the JUnit fail() method available for use within the test class.

intent_record.intent_summary: Import StandardCharsets to prepare for character encoding operations.
intent_record.what: Added an import statement for java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets.
intent_record.how: Makes the StandardCharsets class available for use within the file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add test coverage to ensure directory traversal vulnerabilities (Zip Slip) are prevented during jar extraction.
intent_record.what: Added a new test method `testUnJar2` that creates a JAR file containing a directory traversal entry (`../outside.path`) and asserts that `RunJar.unJar` throws an expected `IOException`.
intent_record.how: Verifies that the unjarring functionality correctly detects and prevents extraction of archive entries that attempt to write outside the intended extraction directory.

grouping_result.intent: Add a security guard to prevent path traversal during jar extraction.
Add static import for JUnit's fail method to support testing.
Import StandardCharsets to prepare for character encoding operations.
Add test coverage to ensure directory traversal vulnerabilities (Zip Slip) are prevented during jar extraction.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add security measures to prevent path traversal (Zip Slip) vulnerabilities during jar extraction.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly implements security guards to prevent path traversal (Zip Slip) vulnerabilities during archive extraction, which perfectly aligns with the described root cause.
Adding a static import for a testing method is a test-related change and does not address the core logic of preventing directory traversal during archive extraction.
Importing StandardCharsets is a general utility or refactoring change and does not address the lack of validation on archive entry names that causes the directory traversal (Zip Slip) vulnerability.
The group adds test coverage specifically for the Zip Slip vulnerability, directly addressing the root cause by ensuring the traversal sequences are properly handled.

candidates.rationale: This line constructs the destination file path directly using the archive entry name without validating if it escapes the target directory, leading to a Zip Slip vulnerability.
Similar to the above, this line directly concatenates the target directory and the unvalidated entry name, allowing directory traversal sequences to write outside the intended target.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement constructs the target file path directly from the archive entry name without verifying if it escapes the intended target directory, which is the root cause of the Zip Slip vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: YARN-2185. Use pipes when localizing archives. Contributed by Miklos Szegedi
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:

Author: Jason Lowe <jlowe@apache.org>
Date:   Wed Jan 31 09:39:43 2018 -0600

    YARN-2185. Use pipes when localizing archives. Contributed by Miklos Szegedi

diff --git a/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/apache/hadoop/util/RunJar.java b/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/apache/hadoop/util/RunJar.java
index 19b51ad692a..89b7d763ec1 100644
--- a/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/apache/ha
bic_trace_step.reason: The added `unJar` method extracts entries directly using `new File(toDir, entry.getName())` without validating if the entry name contains directory traversal sequences (like `../`). This introduces the Zip Slip vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: HADOOP-13068. Clean up RunJar and related test class. (Contributed by Andras Bokor)
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit f343d91ecc0d1c6d9dc9810faf68ec04f7b07c2f
Author: Arpit Agarwal <arp@apache.org>
Date:   Wed May 4 09:49:33 2016 -0700

    HADOOP-13068. Clean up RunJar and related test class. (Contributed by Andras Bokor)

diff --git a/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/apache/hadoop/util/RunJar.java b/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/apache/hadoop/util/RunJar.java
index 52cf05c0477..19b51ad692a 100644
--- a/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/ap
bic_trace_step.reason: The unJar function directly constructs a File object using the archive entry name (new File(toDir, entry.getName());) without validating if the resulting path resolves to a location outside the intended target directory. This leaves the code vulnerable to directory traversal (Zip Slip).

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
HADOOP-4687 Moving src directories on branch


git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/hadoop/core/branches/HADOOP-
bic_trace_step.reason: The 'unJar' method directly constructs a new File using 'entry.getName()' on line 44 without validating whether the resulting path escapes the intended 'toDir' directory. This allows a Zip Slip vulnerability where crafted archive entries with directory traversal sequences (e.g., '../') can write files outside the target directory.
